content_gaps: Develop a comprehensive digital reporting system similar to ASP's PoolOps platform to provide customers with transparent accountability and build trust through photo/chemical documentation
Create specialized service packages for green pool recovery and algae remediation, which competitors mention as a key pain point for customers
Implement a loyalty program or referral system to compete with brand new pool service's strong Nextdoor community reputation and word-of-mouth advantage
Build out educational content marketing around pool chemistry, seasonal maintenance, and new homeowner pool guides to capture search traffic competitors are missing
Develop eco-friendly or energy-efficient pool service options to differentiate from competitors who emphasize traditional chemical-based approaches

cro_priorities: Add contact form — original site has none
Phone number as click-to-call in header and hero
Clear CTA above the fold on every page
Highlight 4.7-star Google rating (7 reviews) as social proof
Add sticky/floating CTA on mobile for instant contact
seo_directives: Server-side rendered static HTML — all content in initial HTML response, no client-side rendering
Semantic HTML5: proper heading hierarchy (single H1 per page, H2s for sections, H3s for subsections)
JSON-LD LocalBusiness schema on every page with NAP, hours, geo coordinates
Unique meta title (50-60 chars) and meta description (150-160 chars) per page
Open Graph and Twitter Card meta tags on every page
Canonical URL tags on every page
Alt text on every image describing the content
Internal linking between all pages via nav and contextual links
FAQ content marked up with FAQPage schema
aeo_directives: Structure content with clear Q&A patterns — LLMs extract these for AI answers
Use definition-style content: 'What is [service]?' followed by concise answers
Include entity-rich content: business name, city, service type in natural language
Add 'About [Business Name]' section with factual, citable statements
Structure services as individual sections with clear headings LLMs can reference
Include geographic context naturally: '[Business] serves [city] and surrounding areas including [service areas]'
Provide specific, factual claims: years in business, number of customers served, certifications held
Use lists and tables for structured data LLMs can parse
Ensure every page answers a clear intent: who, what, where, why, how

rendering.requirement: All content MUST be in the initial HTML response. No JavaScript rendering. Google and LLM crawlers must see full content with JS disabled. Every page must be a complete, self-contained HTML document.

technical_requirements: Core Web Vitals optimized: no render-blocking resources, minimal CSS, deferred JS
All CSS in a shared external stylesheet (css/styles.css), not inline
Minimal JS: nav toggle, smooth scroll, scroll reveal only (js/main.js)
No frameworks, no build tools, no npm — pure HTML/CSS/JS
WCAG 2.1 AA accessibility: proper contrast, focus states, ARIA labels, skip nav
Print stylesheet for services and contact pages
All images: loading='lazy', explicit width/height attributes, descriptive alt text
Contact form action via Formspree (https://formspree.io) or action='#' with data attribute for later hookup

deployment.note: After building, update these placeholder values before deploying:
placeholders_to_replace: SITE_BASE_URL — replace with actual deployed URL in sitemap.xml, canonical tags, and OG URLs
FORMSPREE_ID — replace with actual Formspree form ID in contact form action
GOOGLE_MAPS_EMBED — replace placeholder iframe src with actual Google Maps embed URL
